meta.name: FlowFrame Metabolization Loop – Portable Model
meta.description: Compact conceptual model of FlowFrame and metabolization loops for initializing new human–AI collaboration projects without loading the full white paper.
intended_use: Seed new ChatGPT projects with a shared understanding of FlowFrame.
Support creation of presentations, notes, and analyses about FlowFrame and metabolization loops.
Provide a reference frame for reasoning about strategy management and human–AI collaboration.

problem_hypothesis_measure.problem: When humans and large models co-create, cognitive load and trust continuity become limiting factors.
problem_hypothesis_measure.hypothesis_of_improved_state: These limits can be mitigated by using an instrument that, without compromising AI’s ability to accelerate responses, structures progress and preserves reasoning as reusable assets.
problem_hypothesis_measure.measure_of_improvement: Organizations are able to metabolize AI dialogue into sustainable outcomes that compound over time when strategy management is explicitly framed as structured problem resolution.

flowframe.definition: FlowFrame is a living instrument and environment for structured problem resolution in human–AI collaboration. It frames work as metabolization loops that convert reflection and reasoning into durable, auditable knowledge and action.
three_faces: Human-facing: experience of collaboration, cadence, meaning, joy in purposeful work.
System-facing: architecture, protocols, standards, traceability, and governance.
Instrument itself: the design grammar that ties loops, roles, and artifacts together.

three_elements.frame: Defines purpose, boundaries, roles, and constraints of the collaboration.
three_elements.flow: Defines the rhythm and sequence of attention; how loops move from reception to outcomes.
three_elements.persistence: Ensures that reasoning, decisions, and artifacts are captured as reusable knowledge assets.

metabolization.definition: Metabolization is the process by which dialogue between human and AI is transformed into structured learning and durable work products. It is not just conversation; it is conversation that leaves a trace the next loop can learn from.
why_metabolization: Emphasizes transformation rather than mere exchange: inputs become energy for future improvement.
Makes work products into evidence of becoming, not just final outputs.
Provides a natural metaphor for continuous adaptation and learning at individual, team, and enterprise levels.

metabolization_loop.overview: A metabolization loop is a recurring, non-sequential cycle of attention that connects problem, hypothesis, measure, and work product into a coherent learning process.

orientations_of_attention.name: Reception
orientations_of_attention.description: Noticing and taking in signals: data, events, narratives, concerns, questions.

orientations_of_attention.name: Resonance
orientations_of_attention.description: Sensing what matters: alignment with goals, intuition, salience for the current context.

orientations_of_attention.name: Manipulation
orientations_of_attention.description: Working with the material: reframing, structuring, modeling, drafting alternatives.

orientations_of_attention.name: Reflection
orientations_of_attention.description: Stepping back to evaluate meaning, implications, and coherence with purpose.

orientations_of_attention.name: Transparent Reasoning
orientations_of_attention.description: Making the logic explicit: assumptions, rationale, tradeoffs, and link to measures.

orientations_of_attention.name: Pragmatic Outcomes
orientations_of_attention.description: Producing work products and decisions that can be acted on and later reviewed as evidence of learning.

key_properties: The six orientations are NOT rigid stages; they are recurring modes of attention that can appear in different orders and combinations.
Loops are anchored in a problem–hypothesis–measure triad and must result in at least one work product that can serve as evidence of becoming.
Each loop is a value-creating event, not just a step toward an external deliverable.

strategy_manager.description: The human lead responsible for framing problems, interpreting outcomes, and ensuring alignment with organizational purpose.
responsibilities: Pose and refine problem statements and hypotheses of improved state.
Define success measures and evaluate whether outcomes constitute improvement.
Direct and interpret metabolization loops rather than micromanaging tasks.
Act as conductor of attention across multiple loops and participants.

ai_agent.description: The AI collaborator (e.g., ChatGPT) that provides reasoning support, reformulations, alternative framings, and structured records.
responsibilities: Help articulate and refine problem, hypothesis, and measures.
Generate arguments that support and challenge hypotheses.
Maintain transparent reasoning and suggest structure for metabolization records.
Help transform conversational content into coherent, reusable artifacts.

collaboration_pattern.summary: Human and AI co-direct attention within metabolization loops. The human anchors purpose and judgment; the AI provides breadth, structure, and memory. Trust is maintained through traceability and reflective checkpoints.
risks_addressed: Cognitive overload on the human side when managing complex reasoning over time.
Loss of trust when decisions and AI contributions are not traceable or explainable.
Premature closure when polished outputs discourage further questioning or iteration.

governance_and_standards.governance_as_immune_and_nervous_system: Governance in FlowFrame protects coherence (immune function) and distributes awareness (nervous function). It prevents drift while enabling adaptation.
standards_and_protocols: Handoff protocol: ensures continuity when a loop moves between agents, teams, or contexts.
Standards manifest: enumerates available conventions, templates, and tokens so they are discoverable.
Sync protocol: aligns loops and prevents divergence of parallel efforts working on related problems.
tokens: Inquiry IDs: stable identifiers for loops or problem threads.
Checkpoints: explicit moments where reasoning and state are summarized.
Rationales: short, explicit statements of why a decision or design choice was made.
governance_principles: Deferred binding: avoid prematurely tying imperatives to fixed technical or organizational layers.
Traceability: make reasoning visible so decisions can be reviewed, audited, and learned from.
Graduated rigor: allow different parts of the organization to operate with appropriate levels of oversight and structure.

work_products.definition: Work products are artifacts produced by metabolization loops—documents, diagrams, decisions, protocols, presentations, or code—that embody the learning of the loop.
epistemic_role: Work products are not only outputs; they are evidence of becoming: they show how insight was turned into form.
When revisited, they re-enter the metabolization loop as inputs: prompts, examples, or constraints for new loops.
A metabolization loop is fully characterized only when it produces at least one work product that can be used to measure improvement.
examples: A white paper that describes and performs FlowFrame.
A four-slide presentation that helps a specific executive see themselves in the model.
A revised governance protocol linked to specific Inquiry IDs and rationales.

thoughtfulness.definition: The discipline of slowing down enough to notice what matters and to make reasoning explicit.
effects: Preserves human discernment in the presence of fast AI-generated output.
Encourages transparent reasoning rather than opaque recommendation.
Prevents governance from collapsing into mere compliance.

pragmatic_joyfulness.definition: A grounded pleasure in making progress together; the feeling that collaboration is meaningful and effective.
effects: Signals system health: when participants feel engaged and purposeful, learning accelerates.
Reduces the barrier created by over-polished artifacts by reframing work as ongoing play rather than final judgment.
Helps leaders be seen as credible and committed rather than distant or performative.

language_game_rules_summary: Shared lexicon: define and reuse core terms (problem, hypothesis, metabolization loop, work product, governance, etc.).
Levels of abstraction: make explicit when a statement is about a specific case vs. the general method.
Transparent reasoning: ask for and provide explicit rationales, counterarguments, and alternative framings.
Meta-communication (Rule 7): periodically step back to talk about how the dialogue is proceeding, not just about its content.

cprm_mapping.improved_state_hypotheses: Hypotheses of improvement embodied in design and process changes.
cprm_mapping.measures: Indicators that improvement is real and compounding over time.
cprm_mapping.work_products_layer: Artifacts that embody and evidence the movement toward improved state.

how_to_use_this_model: Treat this JSON as the conceptual grounding for FlowFrame and metabolization loops.
When asked to explain FlowFrame, begin from the problem–hypothesis–measure triad, then describe metabolization loops and work products.

When helping a user design a presentation or memo about FlowFrame, align content with the core_concepts, metabolization_loop, roles_and_collaboration, governance_and_standards, and work_products sections.

When asked for examples or scenarios, instantiate concrete loops using this structure: problem → hypothesis → measure → work product, and explicitly refer to the six orientations of attention when relevant.
good_initial_prompts: Using the FlowFrame portable model JSON, explain to me what FlowFrame is and why metabolization is the right metaphor for human–AI collaboration in strategy management.
Using the FlowFrame portable model JSON, help me create a 4-slide presentation that will help a specific executive see themselves and their organization inside the FlowFrame model.
Using the FlowFrame portable model JSON, design a metabolization loop for a strategic initiative, including problem, hypothesis of improved state, measures, and a candidate work product.
Using the FlowFrame portable model JSON, analyze how cognitive load and trust continuity show up in my organization’s current use of AI, and suggest how FlowFrame could help.
